stories.headline: IRAN RESHUTTERS STRAIT OF HORMUZ AFTER U.S. NAVY SEIZES IRANIAN VESSEL
stories.body: Over the weekend Iran abandoned its April-17 reopening and halted traffic in the Strait after the U.S. Navy boarded an Iranian ship accused of breaking the ceasefire terms. The chokepoint carries ~20% of global oil and ~20% of LNG; the reversal immediately re-prices the war risk premium that had compressed over the prior two weeks.

stories.headline: OIL RIPS BACK: WTI +5.7% TO $87.94, BRENT +5.6% OVER $95
stories.body: Today's move retraces roughly half of Friday's 12% crash. Brent June is back at $95.42; WTI May at $87.94. With March headline CPI already at 3.3% YoY on a 21.2% gasoline spike, even a partial round-trip keeps energy as the dominant swing factor in upcoming prints and into the April 28-29 FOMC.

stories.headline: STOCKS SLIP BUT LOSSES CAPPED AS TRADERS BET ON COMPROMISE
stories.body: S&P 500 -0.30% to ~7,125, Nasdaq -0.40% to ~24,337, Dow -0.10%. 10Y yield fell 4 bps to 4.13% on flight-to-safety even as oil jumped. DXY +0.62% to 98.31; gold -1.19% to $4,809 as positioning unwound into the bid. Defensive rotation was shallow — VIX up only ~2 handles — signaling the tape still expects de-escalation.

stories.headline: FED PATH REPRICED AGAIN; POWELL TERM ENDS MAY 15
stories.body: Fed funds futures now price a ~95% probability of a hold at the April 28-29 FOMC, with the first full cut pushed to September at ~55%. Powell's term expires May 15 — the next Chair will inherit the oil-shock reaction function. The median March dot plot still implies one cut in 2026 from the 3.50-3.75% target range.

stories.headline: BIG TECH Q1 WEEK: TESLA, ALPHABET WEDNESDAY; MICROSOFT, META LATER
stories.body: Tesla reports Wednesday April 22 after the bell — consensus ~$23.1B revenue and $0.40 adjusted EPS, though Q1 deliveries already printed at 358K (-14% QoQ, below 370K consensus). Alphabet also Wednesday. Microsoft and Meta the following week. Margin guidance and AI-capex commentary will anchor sector multiples into month-end.

stories.headline: TRANSFER PORTAL CLOSES TOMORROW; CALEB WILSON DECLARES TOP-5 PICK
stories.body: The 2026 men's basketball portal shuts Tuesday April 21, ending a ~1,000-player window that opened April 7. Top-ranked portal player Flory Bidunga remains uncommitted. Caleb Wilson has declared for the NBA Draft and is projected top-five. Iowa State's Milan Momcilovic (48.7% 3P, best in D1) is dual-tracking — portal plus draft — with the May 28 withdrawal deadline next.

stories.headline: NO. 8 MAGIC UPSET NO. 1 PISTONS 112-101 IN GAME 1; CUNNINGHAM 39
stories.body: Orlando wire-to-wire in Detroit on Sunday — Detroit is the first No. 1 seed in the play-by-play era (since 1997-98) to never hold the lead in a playoff opener. Franz Wagner dropped 11 of his 19 points in the 4th to seal it. Tonight: Raptors-Cavaliers Game 2 (CLE up 1-0 after a 126-113 win), Timberwolves-Nuggets, Hawks-Knicks.

stories.headline: DRAFT THURSDAY IN PITTSBURGH: MENDOZA TO RAIDERS AT NO. 1 CONSENSUS
stories.body: Round 1 kicks off April 23 at 8 PM ET. Indiana QB Fernando Mendoza is the only broadly agreed top-10 pick; the premium talent beyond him — Caleb Downs (S, Ohio St.), Sonny Styles (LB, Ohio St.), Jeremiyah Love (RB, Notre Dame) — sits at non-premium positions, making trade-up incentives for QB-hungry teams unusually thin.

stories.headline: SPRING GAMES WRAP: OHIO STATE DEFENSE 'SCARY GOOD', MICHIGAN QB STRUGGLES
stories.body: This weekend closed the spring-game slate. Chris Henry Jr. went for 4/96 and a 40-yard TD for Ohio State; freshman QB Tavien St. Clair led all OSU passers (9/20, 161 yds). Michigan's presumed starter Bryce Underwood was 3/9 for 22 yards in limited snaps — freshman Tommy Carr outplayed him with 143 passing / 113 rushing.

sports.detail: Upset by No. 8 Magic in Game 1. Cunningham 39 pts / 5 reb / 4 ast on 13-27. Game 2 at DET Wednesday 4/22, 7 PM ET.
Beat Rangers 5-2. Woo 7 IP; Refsnyder HR first pitch of 1st. Next: vs. Athletics Tue 4/21, 6:40 PM PT, T-Mobile Park.
Four picks starting at No. 32. Top needs: RB, edge, CB (Walker gone, Charbonnet out post-surgery). Cliff Avril will announce picks in Rds 2-3.
Practice 7 moved to Seahawks' VMAC this week. 4-star QB Blake Roskopf (2027) committed. Spring Game on deck.
Added Gonzaga wing Steele Venters (4/16) and Virginia Tech C Christian Gurdak. Lost Zoom Diallo to the portal; Steinbach to NBA Draft; Traore post foot surgery.

watchlist: Tesla Q1 earnings Wed 4/22 after the bell — consensus $23.1B rev, $0.40 EPS (Alphabet same day)
NFL Draft Round 1, Thursday 4/23, 8 PM ET in Pittsburgh
College basketball transfer portal closes Tuesday 4/21
FOMC meeting April 28-29 — market pricing ~95% hold, first cut pushed to September
Pistons vs. Magic Game 2, Wednesday 4/22, 7 PM ET (Pistons down 0-1 as No. 1 seed)

deepDive.title: Oil's round-trip and the Fed's supply-shock playbook — why the April 28 meeting hinges on expectations, not crude
content: Crude just round-tripped in three sessions: WTI $94 → $83 on Friday as the Strait reopened, then back to $88 today after Iran reshuttered the chokepoint and the U.S. Navy seized a vessel. The S&P is holding near record highs because the tape has decided this is geopolitical noise rather than a regime change. But the Fed cannot run policy off the tape. With the April 28-29 FOMC one week out, Powell's penultimate meeting before his May 15 term ends, the question has narrowed: is the oil volatility bleeding into expectations, or is it still compartmentalized as a relative-price shock?

The mechanism Fed staff use is textbook. A pure supply shock (oil, food, chips) raises the price level but weakens output — the opposite of an overheating demand shock. Taylor-rule policy says look through it if three conditions hold: inflation expectations stay anchored, wages don't compound the pass-through, and output gap remains soft. Mechanically, energy enters CPI at a 6.8% weight — gasoline alone at 3.2% — and a sustained $10/bbl WTI move shifts headline by roughly 0.3-0.4pp and core by just 0.05-0.10pp over 12 months. Core PCE, which the FOMC actually targets, strips this out by construction.

What most miss is that 'look through' is conditional, not automatic. The 1979-80 Volcker episode is the cautionary tale: headline-driven disinflation plans collapsed when the Iranian revolution's oil spike shifted inflation expectations, forcing a much steeper rate path later. The 2022 analog is gentler — headline CPI peaked at 9.1% in June 2022 on a Brent spike to $127, then retraced to 6.5% when crude rolled over, while core barely moved from 5.9% to 5.7%. The signal the Fed watches is the 5y5y forward breakeven: if that drifts above 2.5%, the supply-shock label stops applying and policy tightens regardless of spot oil.

Today's implication is specific. If 5y5y breakevens stay contained and the April CPI print on May 13 shows core services decelerating (shelter particularly), Powell can deliver a hawkish-hold on April 29 with language acknowledging energy risk without hinting at hikes. Futures agree — they price 95% hold at this meeting and only a ~55% chance of a September cut. The risk is asymmetric: if the breakeven slips higher into the meeting, the committee will need to explicitly drop the cut from the dot plot, which would steepen the curve fast. Chris should watch 5y5y, not WTI — crude is the excuse, expectations are the trigger.
